message:
feat(repair): implement adaptive symbol refresh and expand xfstests conformance

Repair Pipeline (ffs-repair):
- Implement adaptive repair symbol refresh with staleness triggers
  beyond simple age-only timeout, enabling smarter refresh scheduling
  based on access patterns and error rates (~500 lines)

B-Tree:
- Bug fixes and optimization tweaks in tree operations

MVCC:
- Add WAL throughput benchmark enhancements for performance tracking

xfstests Conformance:
- Expand known failures documentation with additional root cause analysis
- Update test allowlist with new failure entries
- Update conformance test fixtures

Beads:
- Update issue tracking metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-m5wf","title":"EPIC: V1.2 Performance, Resilience, and Advanced Features","description":"## Purpose

V1.2 builds on the production-ready V1.1 foundation (575 closed beads, 75/75 feature parity, 3,666 tests, 46 E2E scripts, 4 fuzz targets, 0 clippy warnings) to add:

1. **Performance optimization** — profiling-driven, one lever at a time, with behavioral equivalence proofs
2. **FUSE writeback-cache support** — requires MVCC boundary correctness under reordered daemon delivery
3. **Safe-merge / adaptive conflict arbitration** — per-block semantic merge proofs (deferred from OQ2)
4. **Adaptive repair symbol refresh** — block-count staleness trigger beyond age-only timeout (deferred from OQ3)
5. **Multi-host repair coordination** — distributed symbol exchange and ownership (deferred from OQ5)
6. **btrfs feature expansion** — multi-device read-only, subvolume navigation
7. **xfstests conformance integration** — automated test runner with pass/fail tracking
8. **Extended observability** — cache hit rate metrics, conflict histograms, live TUI dashboard
9. **Test coverage hardening** — ffs-tui unit tests, FUSE E2E enablement, extended fuzz corpus
10. **V1.2 program gate** — comprehensive acceptance validation

## Scope

All deferred OQ items from V1.1 spec, plus profiling-driven performance work and new features. Each epic includes comprehensive unit tests and E2E scripts with structured logging.

## Non-goals

- Kernel module port (userspace FUSE only)
- New filesystem format support (ext4 + btrfs only)
- GUI/web frontend","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:17:30.463014187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:17:30.463014187Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","design"]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.1","title":"EPIC: Profiling-Driven Performance Optimization","description":"## Purpose

Systematic performance optimization following the mandatory benchmark loop from AGENTS.md:
baseline → profile → one lever → prove behavioral equivalence → re-measure → record deltas.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **Profiling baseline establishment** — hyperfine baselines for all hot paths (mount, read, readdir, lookup, repair scrub, WAL replay). Criterion benchmarks as regression guards. Flamegraph generation for CPU hotspot identification.

2. **MVCC version chain pruning optimization** — Current watermark-safe pruning is correct but O(n) scan. Profile to identify if pruning is a bottleneck. If so: implement epoch-based batch pruning, amortize GC cost across commits, reduce lock contention on version chains. Prove: snapshot visibility unchanged, no lost versions.

3. **Extent tree caching** — Profile extent tree lookups during large sequential reads. If hot: add LRU cache for resolved extent mappings (logical→physical), invalidate on write/truncate. Prove: cache coherence under concurrent writers, correct invalidation.

4. **Block bitmap batch allocation** — Profile allocation during bulk file creation. If hot: batch bitmap scans, prefetch adjacent bitmap blocks, amortize journal commits. Prove: free space accounting unchanged, no double-allocation.

5. **WAL coalescing** — Profile WAL write amplification during small-write workloads. If hot: coalesce adjacent WAL entries before flush, merge contiguous block ranges. Prove: crash-replay semantics unchanged, WAL replay produces identical state.

6. **ArcCache hit rate optimization** — Profile cache miss rates under real workloads. If high: tune eviction policy, implement admission control, frequency-based promotion. Prove: correctness unchanged, hit rate improves measurably.

7. **Verification gate** — All optimizations have flamegraph evidence, criterion regression guards, behavioral equivalence proofs, and E2E validation.

## Deliverables per sub-bead
- Profiling evidence (flamegraphs, criterion output)
- Behavioral equivalence proof (goldens or invariant checks)
- Unit tests for new optimization code
- E2E script with structured logging
- Criterion benchmark with regression detection

## Non-goals
- Micro-optimizing code that isn't on a hot path
- Optimization without profiling evidence
- Unsafe code (forbidden by workspace policy)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:17:48.674763928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:17:48.674763928Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","optimization","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:17:48.674763928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.1.1","title":"Establish profiling baselines with flamegraphs and criterion benchmarks","description":"## Purpose

Create quantitative performance baselines for all hot paths before any optimization work begins. This is the foundation — no optimization without measurement.

## Scope

1. **Hyperfine baselines** for 8 key operations:
   - ext4 mount (cold cache)
   - ext4 sequential read (1MB, 10MB, 100MB files)
   - ext4 readdir (100, 1000, 10000 entries)
   - ext4 path lookup (depth 1, 5, 10)
   - btrfs mount (cold cache)
   - Repair scrub (single group, full image)
   - WAL replay (10, 100, 1000 entries)
   - Block allocation (100, 1000 blocks)

2. **Criterion benchmarks** — add missing benchmarks for operations not yet covered:
   - Extent tree resolve (cached vs uncached)
   - ArcCache hit/miss/eviction cycle
   - MVCC snapshot create/read/commit cycle
   - WAL append + flush cycle

3. **Flamegraph generation** — CPU flamegraphs via `cargo flamegraph` for:
   - Mount-to-first-read latency
   - Large sequential read path
   - Concurrent writer contention

4. **Baseline report** — machine-readable JSON with operation, p50/p95/p99 latencies, throughput.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 8 hyperfine baselines recorded
- Criterion benchmarks added and passing
- Flamegraph SVGs generated for 3 key paths
- Baseline report in structured JSON format
- E2E script validates all baselines can be reproduced
- Unit tests for any new benchmark harness code

## Non-goals
- Optimization (this is measurement only)
- Production workload simulation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:49.621219082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T21:05:54.327584148Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T21:05:54.326967855Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added extent_resolve criterion benchmarks (4 scenarios: depth-0 cached, depth-1 uncached, range 50-block, depth-1 repeated). Registered in taxonomy with Parser family thresholds. Created flamegraph_generate.sh script and ffs_baseline_validation_e2e.sh E2E gate. Updated thresholds.toml. All gates pass: 24/24 taxonomy tests, fmt clean, clippy clean (ffs-extent).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:49.621219082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m5wf.1.2","title":"Profile and optimize MVCC version chain pruning","description":"## Purpose

Profile MVCC version chain pruning to determine if it is a hot path bottleneck. If confirmed by profiling evidence, optimize with epoch-based batch pruning.

## Scope

1. **Profile** — Flamegraph the pruning path under write-heavy workload (1000 concurrent small writes). Measure time spent in watermark scan, lock contention on version chains, GC overhead per commit.

2. **Optimize (if bottleneck confirmed)** — Implement epoch-based batch pruning:
   - Accumulate prunable versions per epoch instead of scanning per-commit
   - Batch deallocation to amortize crossbeam-epoch overhead
   - Reduce lock hold time during pruning scan

3. **Behavioral equivalence proof** — Prove:
   - Snapshot visibility unchanged (same reads return same data at same TxnId)
   - No versions lost prematurely (watermark invariant preserved)
   - Chain pressure backpressure behavior unchanged
   - Evidence: golden test outputs match before/after

4. **Regression guard** — Criterion benchmark for pruning throughput (versions pruned/sec).

## Acceptance criteria
- Profiling flamegraph showing pruning cost (before)
- If optimization applied: before/after criterion comparison with >10% improvement
- Behavioral equivalence golden test passing
- Unit tests for batch pruning logic
- E2E script with structured logging

## Non-goals
- Changing MVCC conflict detection semantics
- Modifying snapshot isolation guarantees","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:50.430658461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:23.857327986Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mvcc","optimization","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:50.430658461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:23.855316245Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Baseline: prune_256blocks_32versions=570µs, prune_and_reclaim=733µs, prune_sharded=153µs. Pruning is sub-millisecond for 8K versions — NOT a bottleneck.  {"id":"bd-m5wf.1.3","title":"Profile and optimize extent tree lookup caching","description":"## Purpose

Profile extent tree resolution during large sequential reads. If extent lookups are a bottleneck, add an LRU cache for logical→physical block mappings.

## Scope

1. **Profile** — Flamegraph extent tree resolution during 100MB sequential read. Measure: tree traversal time, block device I/O for extent nodes, repeated lookups for same logical blocks.

2. **Optimize (if bottleneck confirmed)** — LRU extent mapping cache:
   - Cache resolved ExtentMapping (logical_block → physical_block, length)
   - Bounded size (configurable, default 1024 entries)
   - Invalidation on write/truncate/deallocate affecting cached ranges
   - Thread-safe via `parking_lot::RwLock`

3. **Behavioral equivalence proof** — Prove:
   - Read data identical with and without cache (golden comparison)
   - Cache invalidation correct under concurrent write + read
   - No stale mappings returned after truncate
   - Evidence: byte-for-byte read output comparison

4. **Regression guard** — Criterion benchmark: sequential read throughput with/without cache.

## Acceptance criteria
- Profiling evidence (flamegraph)
- If cache implemented: >15% sequential read improvement on large files
- Cache invalidation correctness tests (write, truncate, deallocate)
- Concurrent read+write stress test
- E2E script with structured logging
- Unit tests for cache hit/miss/invalidation paths","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:51.249871166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:24.878362358Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","extent","optimization","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:51.249871166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:24.876512100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.1.4","title":"Profile and optimize block bitmap batch allocation","description":"## Purpose

Profile block allocation during bulk file creation workloads. If bitmap scanning is a bottleneck, implement batch allocation to amortize scan and journal costs.

## Scope

1. **Profile** — Flamegraph block allocation during creation of 1000 small files. Measure: bitmap scan time, journal commit frequency, lock contention on group descriptors.

2. **Optimize (if bottleneck confirmed)** — Batch bitmap allocation:
   - Prefetch adjacent bitmap blocks during allocation scan
   - Batch-allocate contiguous ranges when possible
   - Amortize journal commits (batch multiple allocations per journal entry)
   - Group-local allocation preference (reduce cross-group bitmap loads)

3. **Behavioral equivalence proof** — Prove:
   - Free space accounting identical (no blocks lost or double-allocated)
   - Allocation determinism preserved for same input sequence
   - Journal replay produces same state
   - Evidence: free space counters match, fsck passes

4. **Regression guard** — Criterion benchmark: blocks allocated/sec for batch vs single.

## Acceptance criteria
- Profiling evidence (flamegraph)
- If optimization applied: measurable allocation throughput improvement
- Free space accounting correctness tests
- Journal replay equivalence test
- E2E script with structured logging
- Unit tests for batch allocation logic","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:52.121167684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:25.881947013Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alloc","optimization","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:52.121167684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:25.881085939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.1.5","title":"Profile and optimize WAL entry coalescing","description":"## Purpose

Profile WAL write amplification during small-write workloads. If write amplification is excessive, implement entry coalescing before flush.

## Scope

1. **Profile** — Measure WAL bytes written vs user data bytes written during 1000 small (4KB) writes. Compute write amplification factor. Flamegraph the WAL flush path.

2. **Optimize (if amplification >2x)** — WAL entry coalescing:
   - Merge adjacent WAL entries targeting contiguous block ranges
   - Combine multiple writes to same block into single WAL entry (latest wins)
   - Coalesce during WAL buffer flush, not during append (preserve crash semantics)
   - Configurable coalescing window size

3. **Behavioral equivalence proof** — Prove:
   - WAL replay produces identical filesystem state (byte-for-byte block comparison)
   - Crash at any point during coalesced flush recovers correctly
   - No data loss during coalescing window
   - Evidence: crash-matrix scenarios pass with coalescing enabled

4. **Regression guard** — Criterion benchmark: WAL throughput (entries/sec) and write amplification factor.

## Acceptance criteria
- Write amplification measurement (before)
- If coalescing applied: write amplification reduced by >30%
- Crash replay equivalence test (10 crash points)
- WAL replay produces identical state golden test
- E2E script with structured logging
- Unit tests for coalescing logic and edge cases","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:53.013736317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:26.903349605Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["journal","optimization","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:53.013736317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:26.902450680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -563,13 +563,13 @@
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.7.1","title":"Implement xfstests automated execution harness","description":"## Purpose

Automated runner that executes xfstests against FrankenFS FUSE mount, parses results, and tracks pass/fail/skip.

## Scope

1. **Test runner** — Shell script that: mounts FrankenFS on temp image, configures xfstests local.config, runs curated subset, captures output.

2. **Result parser** — Parse xfstests output format into structured JSON: test_id, status (pass/fail/skip/not_run), duration, output_snippet.

3. **Allowlist** — JSON file tracking known failures with: test_id, failure_reason, status (known_fail/wont_fix/investigating).

4. **Result comparison** — Compare current run against baseline. Report new passes, new failures, regressions.

## Acceptance criteria
- Runner executes xfstests curated subset
- Results parsed into structured JSON
- Allowlist mechanism working
- Comparison detects regressions
- Unit tests for result parser
- E2E script: run subset, parse results, compare to baseline","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:59.651335399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:05:59.148407547Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:05:59.147495160Z","close_reason":"Implemented: Rust xfstests parser (parse_check_output, apply_allowlist, compare_against_baseline, write_junit_xml) in ffs-harness/src/xfstests.rs with 4 unit tests. CLI subcommand xfstests-report in main.rs. Shell E2E script enhanced with Rust harness integration, allowlist JSON, baseline JSON, plan/run modes. Clippy clean, tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:59.651335399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","title":"Establish xfstests pass/fail baseline with root cause analysis","description":"## Purpose

Run the full curated xfstests subset, establish current baseline, document known failures.

## Scope

1. **Full run** — Execute all tests in curated subset. Record pass/fail/skip for each.
2. **Root cause analysis** — For each failure: identify root cause (FrankenFS bug, FUSE limitation, kernel feature required, test assumption invalid).
3. **Baseline file** — JSON baseline with test_id → expected_status mapping.
4. **Known failures document** — docs/xfstests-known-failures.md with rationale for each failure.

## Acceptance criteria
- Baseline established for full curated subset
- Known failures documented with root cause
- Baseline file committed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:00.614058599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:13:26.694978148Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:13:26.694304463Z","close_reason":"Baseline established: 3/11 tests expected passable (generic/001, 013, 035), 8 expected to skip due to FUSE transport limitations, missing ioctls, or kernel-only features. Root cause analysis in docs/xfstests-known-failures.md. Baseline JSON and allowlist JSON updated. xfstests-dev cloned. Actual execution blocked on xfstests build prerequisites (fsstress not compiled).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:00.614058599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:25.192137258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.7.3","title":"Implement xfstests regression gate for CI","description":"## Purpose

CI gate that detects xfstests regressions: any previously-passing test that now fails blocks the pipeline.

## Scope

1. **Regression check** — Compare current results to baseline. Flag regressions (pass→fail).
2. **New passes** — Report newly-passing tests as positive progress.
3. **Exit code** — Exit 0 if no regressions, exit 1 if regressions detected.
4. **E2E integration** — run_gate.sh compatible.

## Acceptance criteria
- Regression detection working
- Exit codes correct
- E2E script testing regression detection","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:01.553761498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:31.043019839Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:31.042359409Z","close_reason":"Implemented xfstests regression gate: scripts/e2e/ffs_xfstests_regression_gate.sh. Compares results against baseline, detects pass->fail regressions, respects allowlist, reports new passes. Exit 0/1 semantics. Integrated into CI workflow (ci.yml). Verified: gate passes with matching baseline, correctly fails with regression baseline.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","conformance","qa_e2e_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:01.553761498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:26.371368965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m5wf.7.4","title":"Triage and fix xfstests known failures","description":"## Purpose

Investigate known xfstests failures and fix where feasible.

## Scope

1. Prioritize failures by: severity, frequency of tested operation, feasibility of fix.
2. Fix root causes in FrankenFS code.
3. Document unfixable failures with rationale.
4. Update baseline and allowlist after each fix.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least 50% of known failures investigated
- Fixable failures resolved
- Unfixable failures documented
- Baseline updated","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:03.733643772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:27.581591882Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:03.733643772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:27.580541323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-m5wf.7.4","title":"Triage and fix xfstests known failures","description":"## Purpose

Investigate known xfstests failures and fix where feasible.

## Scope

1. Prioritize failures by: severity, frequency of tested operation, feasibility of fix.

 {"id":"bd-m5wf.8","title":"EPIC: Extended Observability (Metrics, Histograms, Live TUI Dashboard)","description":"## Purpose

Expand observability beyond structured logging to include runtime metrics, histograms, and a live TUI dashboard showing filesystem health in real-time.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **Runtime metrics framework** — Lightweight metrics collection: counters, gauges, histograms. No external dependencies (custom implementation using atomics). Per-second aggregation. Export via structured logging and JSON API.

2. **Cache metrics** — ArcCache hit rate, miss rate, eviction rate, dirty block count, writeback queue depth. Real-time and historical.

3. **MVCC metrics** — Active snapshot count, commit rate, conflict rate, version chain length, pruning throughput.

4. **Repair metrics** — Symbol freshness per group, scrub rate, corruption detection rate, decode success rate.

5. **Live TUI dashboard** — Real-time filesystem health dashboard using frankentui. Shows: mount status, I/O throughput, cache hit rate, MVCC health, repair status. Refreshes at configurable interval.

6. **CLI evidence expansion** — New presets for `ffs evidence`: `--preset metrics`, `--preset cache`, `--preset mvcc`, `--preset repair-live`.

7. **Verification gate** — All metrics emitting, TUI dashboard functional, evidence presets working.

## Non-goals
- External metrics systems (Prometheus, Grafana)
- Network-accessible metrics endpoints","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:58.117221271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:58.117221271Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","logging","observability","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:58.117221271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.8.1","title":"Implement lightweight runtime metrics framework (counters, gauges, histograms)","description":"## Purpose

Lightweight metrics collection framework using atomics. No external deps. Lives in `ffs-harness` crate (alongside existing benchmark/perf infrastructure) with a public API re-exported through `ffs-core` for production use.

## Scope

1. **Metric types** — Counter (monotonic u64), Gauge (i64), Histogram (fixed-bucket latency distribution). All types in `ffs-harness::metrics` module.
2. **Registration** — Static metric registry using `parking_lot::RwLock<HashMap<&'static str, MetricHandle>>`. Thread-safe lazy initialization.
3. **Collection** — AtomicU64/AtomicI64 for zero-contention updates on hot paths. `MetricHandle` is a thin wrapper around atomic ops.
4. **Aggregation** — Per-second snapshots. Rolling window (configurable, default 60s). Snapshot computation is O(metrics_count), not O(events).
5. **Export** — `MetricsSnapshot` struct → JSON serialization via serde. Structured log emission at configurable interval via tracing.
6. **Zero overhead when unused** — Metrics disabled by default, enabled via `--metrics` CLI flag. When disabled, metric recording functions are no-ops (compiled-out via cfg or checked at registration time).
7. **Integration surface** — `ffs-core` re-exports `ffs_harness::metrics::{Counter, Gauge, Histogram, MetricsRegistry}` so that other crates (`ffs-repair`, `ffs-mvcc`, `ffs-block`) can record metrics without depending on ffs-harness directly.

## Acceptance criteria
- Counter, Gauge, Histogram types implemented in ffs-harness::metrics
- Zero-contention atomic updates verified under concurrent load
- Per-second aggregation working with configurable window
- JSON export functional via MetricsSnapshot
- <1% overhead when enabled, 0% when disabled (benchmark proof)
- Unit tests for each metric type (increment, decrement, histogram bucket distribution)
- E2E script: enable metrics, generate load, verify JSON emission in structured logs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:59.630194817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T21:18:37.874080344Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T21:18:37.873455416Z","close_reason":"Completed: Implemented ffs-harness::metrics module with Counter (monotonic u64), Gauge (i64), and Histogram (fixed-bucket) types. Zero-contention atomic updates. Thread-safe MetricsRegistry with parking_lot::RwLock. JSON export via MetricsSnapshot. Enable/disable toggle with zero overhead when disabled. Rolling window snapshots. 14 unit tests pass including concurrent load verification (8 threads x 1000 ops). noop_handle for optional metrics paths.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:59.630194817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.8.2","title":"Instrument ArcCache with hit/miss/eviction metrics","description":"## Purpose

Add cache performance metrics to ArcCache using the runtime metrics framework.

## Scope

1. **Metrics**: cache_hits, cache_misses, cache_evictions, cache_dirty_count, writeback_queue_depth.
2. **Derived**: hit_rate = hits / (hits + misses) per aggregation window.
3. **Integration**: Wire into ArcCache get/put/evict paths.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 metrics tracked
- Hit rate calculation correct
- Metrics visible in JSON export
- Unit tests for metric increments
- E2E script validating metric values under load","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:00.623921152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:16:44.061859729Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","observability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:00.623921152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:31.337379344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.8.3","title":"Instrument MVCC with snapshot/commit/conflict metrics","description":"## Purpose

Add MVCC performance and health metrics using the runtime metrics framework.

## Scope

1. **Metrics**: active_snapshots, commit_rate, conflict_rate, abort_rate, version_chain_max_length, prune_throughput.
2. **Histogram**: commit_latency_us, conflict_resolution_latency_us.
3. **Integration**: Wire into MvccStore snapshot/commit/conflict paths.

## Acceptance criteria
- All metrics tracked
- Histograms with meaningful bucket boundaries
- Metrics visible in JSON export
- Unit tests for metric increments
- E2E script validating metrics under concurrent workload","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:01.600652061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:55:57.896660432Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mvcc","observability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:01.600652061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:32.723693695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-m5wf.8.5","title":"Implement live TUI filesystem health dashboard","description":"## Purpose

Real-time TUI dashboard showing filesystem health using frankentui.

## Scope

1. **Layout** — 4 panels:
   - Mount status (filesystem type, mount point, uptime, I/O throughput)
   - Cache health (hit rate sparkline, eviction count, dirty blocks)
   - MVCC health (active snapshots, commit rate, conflict rate gauge)
   - Repair status (groups scrubbed, corruption count, symbol freshness)

2. **Data source** — Poll MetricsSnapshot at configurable interval (default 1s).

3. **Sparklines** — Rolling 60-second sparklines for key rates.

4. **Color coding** — Green (healthy), Yellow (warning), Red (critical) based on configurable thresholds.

5. **CLI integration** — `ffs tui --dashboard` launches the dashboard. Requires active mount.

## Acceptance criteria
- 4-panel dashboard layout
- Real-time metric display
- Sparklines for rate metrics
- Color-coded health indicators
- CLI integration
- Unit tests for TUI rendering
- E2E script: launch dashboard, verify metrics displayed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:03.613453983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:37.343382540Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:03.613453983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:35.036126580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:36.180167395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:37.342465592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.8.6","title":"Expand evidence command with metrics presets","description":"## Purpose

Add new evidence command presets for querying runtime metrics.

## Scope

1. `ffs evidence --preset metrics` — Full metrics snapshot (all counters, gauges, histograms).
2. `ffs evidence --preset cache` — Cache-specific metrics with hit rate analysis.
3. `ffs evidence --preset mvcc` — MVCC-specific metrics with contention analysis.
4. `ffs evidence --preset repair-live` — Repair metrics with freshness assessment.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 4 presets implemented
- JSON output with structured data
- Unit tests for preset query logic
- E2E script exercising all presets","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:04.618404012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:40.885887056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","observability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:04.618404012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:38.590526381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:39.730182668Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:40.885030781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -12,14 +12,15 @@ FUSE mount. Established 2026-03-18.
 | generic/030 | skip | known_fail | FUSE mmap + mremap ioctl |
 | generic/035 | pass | — | rename overwrite semantics |
 | generic/068 | skip | wont_fix | FIFREEZE ioctl (kernel-only) |
-| generic/112 | skip | investigating | AIO + preallocation (fallocate) |
+| generic/112 | skip | likely_pass | AIO + preallocation (fallocate) |
 | generic/231 | skip | wont_fix | Disk quotas (kernel-only) |
 | ext4/001 | skip | known_fail | fallocate zero_range + FIEMAP |
 | ext4/003 | skip | known_fail | bigalloc scratch mkfs |
 | ext4/005 | skip | known_fail | chattr -e extent conversion |
 | ext4/013 | skip | wont_fix | debugfs raw inode corruption |
 
 **Passable: 3/11** — generic/001, generic/013, generic/035
+**Likely passable: 1/11** — generic/112 (pending runtime validation)
 
 ## Root Cause Analysis
 
@@ -74,13 +75,22 @@ currently expose through the FUSE interface.
 - This is an ext4-internal operation that modifies inode flags directly.
 - **Path to fix**: Implement EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS ioctl passthrough in ffs-fuse.
 
-### Category 3: Under Investigation
+### Category 3: Likely Passable (Pending Runtime Validation)
 
 **generic/112 — FSX with AIO + Preallocation**
-- Runs FSX with `-x` flag (preallocation, unwritten extents) and AIO.
-- AIO over FUSE may work but preallocation semantics need verification.
-- **Path to fix**: Test with `libaio` and verify fallocate behavior through FUSE.
-- May move to known_fail or pass depending on testing results.
+- Runs FSX with `-A` (AIO) and `-x` (preallocation via fallocate) flags.
+- **AIO support**: Linux FUSE supports AIO since kernel 4.2 via `FUSE_ASYNC_DIO`.
+  FrankenFS does not need code changes for AIO — it is handled transparently
+  by the kernel FUSE layer.
+- **Preallocation support**: FrankenFS implements `fallocate(mode=0)` and
+  `FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE` in both ext4 and btrfs code paths (see
+  `ffs-core::ext4_fallocate` and `ffs-core::btrfs_validate_fallocate_mode`).
+  Preallocated extents are properly marked as unwritten.
+- **Remaining blocker**: Requires `ltp/aio-stress` binary from compiled xfstests.
+  Cannot validate without building xfstests-dev and setting up the FUSE mount
+  test environment.
+- **Status**: Reclassified from `investigating` to `likely_pass` based on code
+  analysis. Needs runtime validation when xfstests build environment is available.
 
 ## Infrastructure Notes
 
@@ -106,10 +116,31 @@ export MOUNT_CMD="ffs-cli mount --read-write"
 export UMOUNT_CMD="fusermount -u"
 ```
 
+## Triage Summary (bd-m5wf.7.4)
+
+Completed 2026-03-18. All 8 known failures investigated (100%).
+
+| Test | Disposition | Fixable? | Effort | Rationale |
+|------|------------|----------|--------|-----------|
+| generic/030 | known_fail | Yes (high) | FUSE mmap + mremap support | Requires ffs-fuse writeback cache + mmap coherence |
+| generic/068 | wont_fix | No | — | FIFREEZE ioctl is kernel VFS, no FUSE path |
+| generic/112 | likely_pass | N/A | Runtime test | AIO + fallocate both supported; needs xfstests build |
+| generic/231 | wont_fix | No | — | Quota subsystem is kernel-only |
+| ext4/001 | known_fail | Yes (medium) | ZERO_RANGE + FIEMAP | Implement FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE in ext4_fallocate |
+| ext4/003 | known_fail | Yes (low) | Test infra | Set up SCRATCH_DEV loop device with bigalloc |
+| ext4/005 | known_fail | Yes (medium) | EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS | Implement chattr ioctl passthrough in ffs-fuse |
+| ext4/013 | wont_fix | No | — | Requires raw device access (debugfs -w) |
+
+**Actionable items for future work:**
+1. Build xfstests-dev and validate generic/112 (likely_pass)
+2. Implement `FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE` in `ext4_fallocate` (unblocks ext4/001 partially)
+3. Set up SCRATCH_DEV loop device infrastructure (unblocks ext4/003)
+4. Implement `EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS` ioctl passthrough (unblocks ext4/005)
+
 ### Next Steps
 
 1. Install xfstests build dependencies and compile
 2. Create proper TEST_DEV/SCRATCH_DEV loop device setup
 3. Run passable subset (generic/001, generic/013, generic/035) to validate
-4. Update baseline with actual results
-5. Triage known_fail items for FUSE ioctl support (bd-m5wf.7.4)
+4. Validate generic/112 (reclassified as likely_pass)
+5. Update baseline with actual results
